title: Community Relations: Advertising and Fundraising
description: This policy governs how advertising and fundraising activities are handled within the school district's community relations. It sets standards for what types of advertising are acceptable, including alignment with district values and compliance with related local policies.

timeline_local.summary: This update to Conroe ISD's GKB (Local) Community Relations: Advertising and Fundraising policy adds a new requirement that advertising be rejected if it is not aligned and consistent with district values and in compliance with EMB Local, EFA Local, EFB Local, and EMI Local policies. No language was removed.
changes_added: Advertising shall be rejected if not aligned and consistent with district values and in compliance with EMB Local, EFA Local, EFB Local, and EMI Local.
timeline_local.notes: The red underlined text on page 2 is clearly identifiable. No red strikethrough text was present in the document. The cover page title reads "Discuss and Possibly Adopt Revisions to Board Policy GKB (Local)" which maps to action = 'adopted'.

elements.element: Rejection of advertising not aligned with district values and compliance with EMB Local, EFA Local, EFB Local, and EMI Local policies
elements.supporting_quote: All powers and duties not specifically delegated by statute to the agency or to the State Board of Education are reserved for the trustees
elements.local_additions: District chose to establish specific advertising content standards tied to internal policy alignment and district values; no statute mandates or expressly authorizes this particular advertising rejection standard — it is an exercise of the board's reserved general governance authority

compliance_summary: New Advertising Standard: The district added a rule that allows it to reject advertising that does not align with district values or comply with four related internal policies (EMB Local, EFA Local, EFB Local, and EMI Local). This creates a clear, written standard for what kinds of advertising can be turned down.

Board Authority Used: No state law required or specifically authorized this change — the district made it on its own using its general authority to govern local school matters under Texas Education Code §11.151. School boards in Texas have broad power to set policies like this when state law does not address the specific topic.

No Legal Challenges Found: There are no known court cases or legal disputes connected to this type of advertising policy change. The addition reflects a local governance decision rather than a response to litigation.

timeline_local.summary: This update to Conroe ISD's GKB (Local) policy on Community Relations: Advertising and Fundraising adds a new sentence requiring that advertising be rejected if it is not aligned and consistent with district values and in compliance with four referenced local policies (EMB, EFA, EFB, and EMI Local).
changes_added: Advertising shall be rejected if not aligned and consistent with district values and in compliance with EMB Local, EFA Local, EFB Local, and EMI Local.
timeline_local.notes: The added text appears in red underlined font on page 2 of the document, immediately following the existing list of five advertising rejection criteria. No red strikethrough (removed) text was observed in the redline. The cover page title heading reads "Discuss Revisions to Board Policy GKB (Local)," indicating a first reading.
